subject: english
chapter_title: The Day the River Spoke (Learning Together)

main_sections.title: The Day the River Spoke
main_sections.prose_summary: Jahnavi is a bright girl of nearly ten living in a coastal village in India. Her parents work on the farm, and she has been kept home to mind her younger siblings — first Ramu, then little Appu — while her brother Gopi (whom she calls Ettan, meaning 'Elder brother') and neighbour Meena attend school. Sitting by the river one day, weeping silently as a kingfisher swoops and a lizard basks, Jahnavi hears a sleepy, murmuring voice that turns out to be the River itself. The River, gently personified throughout, draws out her sorrow: she has never been allowed to go to school, is frightened it is too late, and yet burns with curiosity — she wants to know why spiders are yellow in yellow flowers, why bamboo trees rustle, why the moon follows the same path, why fish in the field turn to frogs. The River, with quiet wit, reminds her that little girls can do as much as little boys, and advises her simply to slip into the school one morning and see what happens. Jahnavi summons courage and does exactly that, creeping into the back row with sleeping Appu on her shoulder. The teacher notices her, learns she is Gopi's sister, and promises to speak to her father. That evening Jahnavi watches the teacher visit their home; her father scratches his cheek in worry while the teacher persuades him. Her mother, who once longed for school herself but was denied by her own mother, warmly supports Jahnavi's wish, saying girls should learn as much as they want. Jahnavi resolves that when she grows up she will be a teacher, going house to house to bring all the village girls to school. The next morning she runs to the river to share her triumph — 'I did it! I was scared, but I did it!' — and hears the river chuckle and promise to tell her all about the ships that sail the sea. The story is an extract adapted from Kamala Nair's 'The Day the River Spoke'. Its central themes are the right to education, gender equality, self-belief, and the power of nature as both witness and counsel.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read. I Close your eyes and quietly listen to the sounds around you for a minute. Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.

question_bank.stem: Are they trying to tell you something?
Do you think the things around you also listen to you all day long?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read. II Jahnavi was a bright young girl who lived with her parents and three brothers in a coastal village in India. Her parents worked on the farm. She had a dream. Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.

question_bank.stem: What could that dream be?
How could she make her dream come true?
What is your dream? What can you do to make your dream come true?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss (after Section I of the prose). Answer the questions and share with the teacher.

question_bank.stem: What was Jahnavi's dream? Was it important to her? Why?
Do you think the river can help her in fulfilling her dream? How?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss (after Section II of the prose). Complete the table given below with Jahnavi's questions in Column 1 and the River's answers in Column 2. Check your answers with the teacher.

question_bank.stem: Complete the table with Jahnavi's questions and the River's answers.
question_bank.table:
Column 1 (Jahnavi's questions)|Column 2 (River's answers)
...why the moon always comes from behind the hills…?|
|… little girls can do as much as little boys.
|Big boats, so big that they can take hundreds of people, and they sail along the sea with lights that shine all night.
Will they come here?|

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — I. Extract 1: Read the extract 'You shouldn't cry, you know...I've got to hurry to reach the sea, you know.' and answer the questions that follow.

question_bank.stem: Complete the sentence with the correct option from those given in the brackets. The tone of the River in the given extract is __________________. (assuring/sympathising)
Select a phrase from the extract which shows that Jahnavi was a frequent visitor to the spot.

Choose the correct option to complete the sentence. The use of an exclamation mark at the end of the line, 'It couldn't be the river!' expresses __________________. A. excitement  B. hesitation  C. irritation  D. disbelief
Choose three qualities of the River highlighted in the extract from the words in the box given below: affectionate  forgiving  thoughtful  impatient  kind-hearted

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — I. Extract 2: Read the extract 'Can I do something?...They'd chase me out.' and answer the questions that follow.

question_bank.stem: Complete the sentence with an appropriate reason. According to the River, there was no difference between girls and boys because __________________.
Identify whether the following statement is true or false. The River encourages Jahnavi to have faith in herself and fulfill her desire to go to school.

Choose the correct option to complete the sentence. When the River suggests that Jahnavi should 'slip along', it means that she should move __________________. A. quickly  B. casually  C. quietly  D. confidently
What does the repetition of the phrase I couldn't, tell us?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — II. Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: Why does the writer describe different aspects of nature in great detail at the beginning of the story? Why do you think the writer does this?
How did the River know so much about Jahnavi?
Jahnavi says, 'And I'm so old now, they'll never let me go.' What can you infer about Jahnavi and the school from this line?
Why did the River laugh when Jahnavi said she would be scared at school?
Why should Jahnavi follow the River's advice?
How does the conversation between Jahnavi and the River make the story more appealing?
What is the main message that the writer intends to convey?

listening.transcript_text:
Speaker (i) (Father): Should girls attend school? Yes. I ensure both my son and daughter get equal opportunities. Education gives freedom to make choices — whether my daughter wants to work in an organisation or be a homemaker, it should be her choice, and the same for my son.
Speaker (ii) (Grandmother): I went to school, but many girls then could not, because some village elders felt girls should help in the house and care for younger siblings. I am happy the situation has changed today.
Speaker (iii) (Teacher): School prepares students for meaningful participation in the world of work — learning hands-on skills, developing respect for head, hands and heart, valuing the dignity of labour, and understanding vocational choices.
Speaker (iv) (Student): I like school. By coming to school and reading books, I learn about things from all over the world, and I have friends with whom I can share my feelings.
Speaker (v) (Principal): Everyone should attend school. Girls are doing very well. Students should attend regularly and take part in every activity, whether it is for girls or boys. The idea is to focus on learning.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: I Read the words given in the box below. You will listen to five people speak about school life. As you listen, circle the words that are used by the speakers. There are two words you do not need. Words: lawyer  desks  homemaker  principal  posters  newspapers  marriage  advertisements  careers  books
II You will once again listen to the five speakers. As you listen, match each statement 1–7 to each speaker (i)–(v). There are two statements that you do not need.

question_bank.stem: Match each statement to each speaker. Statements: 1. Promotes active participation for all in school activities. 2. Comments on how school makes learning Mathematics joyful. 3. Believes in the power of education that leads to freedom of choice. 4. Shares that school also means companionship. 5. Understands that boys and girls have different abilities. 6. Persuades the parent to grant permission to attend school. 7. Believes that learning skills is not gender-based.
question_bank.table:
Statement|Speaker No.
1. Promotes active participation for all in school activities|
2. Comments on how school makes learning Mathematics joyful|
3. Believes in the power of education that leads to freedom of choice|
4. Shares that school also means companionship|
5. Understands that boys and girls have different abilities|
6. Persuades the parent to grant permission to attend school|
7. Believes that learning skills is not gender-based|

tasks_verbatim.task_text: I Read the sentence given below. 'Dear, dear! What's the matter? … You shouldn't cry, you know. And you really shouldn't be scared, when you have been coming here to see me every day, well, almost every day.' The River spoke to Jahnavi in a 'sleepy' voice. Work in pairs and take turns to speak the same sentence in 'other' voices. Some hints are given below: happy  surprised  angry  scared  sad  worried
II Jahnavi asks the River for advice and the River gives advice. Read the given situations and work in pairs to ask for and give advice. Take turns to change your roles. Use the prompts given below.

question_bank.stem: Situation (i): You wish to play football at school but it has an all-boys' team only.
Situation (ii): You have been unable to score well in Mathematics due to nervousness during exams.
Situation (iii): You really enjoy music classes but do not get enough time to practise for it.
Situation (iv): You cannot see the blackboard clearly from the last bench.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Observing Nature. Take some time to observe the world around you. Over the next few days, pay attention to the colours, shapes, patterns, and textures found in nature. Look closely at the plants, trees, flowers, birds, and even the sky. Make detailed notes of what you observe, focusing on textures, movements, and how light plays with these elements. Now, write a descriptive paragraph based on what you've observed. Highlight the colours, shapes, textures, and unique details that made an impression on you. Give a title to your paragraph. Use the Ideas and Prompts table as a guide.

question_bank.stem: Write a descriptive paragraph based on your observation of nature. Give a title to your paragraph. Use the prompts: Every day, I see… / I find it very interesting that… / I like… because… / I feel… as… / I want to tell you that… / My advice to all is…

tasks_verbatim.task_text: I You have learnt that naming words are called nouns and only proper nouns begin with a capital letter. Why has the author used a capital letter for the 'River' even when it is a common noun? (Clue: Has the River been given qualities of a human being?)

question_bank.stem: Why has the author used a capital letter for the 'River' even when it is a common noun? (Clue: Has the River been given qualities of a human being?)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: II There are a few words related to sound in the text. Pick the words from the text and write them next to the correct definition.

question_bank.stem: Pick sound words from the text and write them next to the correct definition: 1. made a high-pitched piercing sound  2. to cause liquid to strike or fall on something  3. a low continuous background noise  4. cry with loud uncontrollable gasps  5. to make a soft dry sound, like paper or leaves moving  6. took a short quick breath through the mouth due to surprise, pain or shock  7. shaking rapidly to make continuous short, sharp sounds  8. making a lot of noise  9. made a long, high cry, usually because of pain or sadness
question_bank.table:
Definition|Sound word
made a high-pitched piercing sound|
to cause liquid to strike or fall on something|
a low continuous background noise|
cry with loud uncontrollable gasps|
to make a soft dry sound, like paper or leaves moving|
took a short quick breath through the mouth due to surprise, pain or shock|
shaking rapidly to make continuous short, sharp sounds|
making a lot of noise|
made a long, high cry, usually because of pain or sadness|

question_bank.stem: Now, fill in the blanks with suitable sound words from the exercise. The classroom grew (i) _______ as the students noticed the teacher carrying the answer papers. As the teacher turned the pages with a (ii) _______, the children (iii) _______, wondering what comments awaited. Someone (iv) _______ in frustration, and the (v) _______of the students steadily increased. Finally, when the teacher praised the students for their excellent performance, the students (vi) _______ with excitement.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: III The word 'catamaran' is used in the text. The word originates from Tamil language 'kattu maram' meaning 'tied wood'. Find out the meanings of these words. From which Indian language these words were borrowed. An example has been given.

question_bank.stem: Find out the meanings of these words and from which Indian language they were borrowed: 1. karma (Sanskrit)  2. sahib  3. chutney  4. guru  5. verandah  6. jungle  7. areca  8. palanquin

tasks_verbatim.task_text: IV Chandu, the fisherman, has a catamaran that the River preferred to a ship. Given below are some boats that are used in different parts of our country: 1. canoe  2. catamaran  3. coracle  4. shikara

question_bank.stem: Which one would you like to travel in?
Make a colourful drawing of a boat that you would like to travel in.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: V Solve the crossword puzzle based on words related to water transport. Across: 1. a large vessel used for transporting goods or passengers by sea  2. a small vessel for travelling on water, typically propelled by oars, sails, or an engine  3. a flat floating structure for travelling across water  4. a boat or ship driven by steam. Down: 5. a small, light, narrow boat, pointed at both ends and moved using a paddle  6. a boat with a powerful engine which can change direction easily and is used to pull large ships into and out of port  7. a boat with sails and sometimes an engine, used for either racing or travelling for pleasure, rhymes with 'cot'

question_bank.stem: Solve the crossword puzzle based on words related to water transport.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: VI Prepositions are words that indicate the relationship between nouns or pronouns and other elements in a sentence. They show direction, location, time or logical relationships between ideas. Now, fill in the blanks with suitable prepositions given within the brackets.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with suitable prepositions given within the brackets. Jahnavi takes the River's advice and goes to school. On her first day 1. _________ (at/in/on) school, Jahnavi stood nervously 2. _________ (in/outside/above) the gate, clutching her bag close to her. The bell rang and she hurried 3. _________ (outside/through/inside) the bustling classroom. She found a seat 4. _________ (among/between/from) two friendly classmates. The teacher greeted them warmly and started the lesson 5. _________ (about/in/for) numbers. Jahnavi listened attentively, feeling excited 6. _________ (at/about/for) the new adventure ahead.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: VII Words like on, off, up, down can function both as preposition and as adverb. Fill in the blanks with prepositions or adverbs.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with prepositions or adverbs: 1. The cat jumped ___________ the table. (preposition)  2. The children played ___________ until dark. (adverb)  3. She quickly ran ___________ the park. (preposition)  4. The helicopter was hovering ___________. (adverb)  5. We were just walking ___________, chatting. (adverb)  6. After thoroughly wiping his shoes, he stepped ___________. (adverb)  7. She drove slowly ___________ the sharp curve. (preposition)  8. He placed the keys ___________ the mat before leaving. (preposition)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: I Discuss why rivers in India are considered to be sacred and most of them are referred to as female. Some rivers are referred to as male, for example, the Brahmaputra and the Sone.

II Whole Class Activity: 1. India is a multilingual country, and many of us can speak two to three languages easily. Make a list of the languages that everyone in the class can speak and display it on a chart paper in the classroom. 2. In which language do you enjoy reading and learning the most?

question_bank.stem: Make a list of the languages that everyone in the class can speak and display it on a chart paper in the classroom.
In which language do you enjoy reading and learning the most?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: III Read the poster given below and answer the questions that follow. The poster reads: 'The Happiness of a Nation lies in the Dignity of its Daughters'

question_bank.stem: What are the things you can observe in the poster?
Why do you think the girls look happy?
Dignity means 'self-respect'. What is the relation between 'dignity' and 'happiness'?
How does a Nation prosper when its daughters are happy?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: IV The Government has launched many schemes for the education of the girl child. Some of them are 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao', 'Balika Samridhi Yojana', 'Samagra Shiksha Scheme–Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas', etc. Find out more such schemes from the internet or from your teacher and spread awareness in your neighbourhood.

question_bank.stem: Find out more government schemes for the education of the girl child from the internet or from your teacher and spread awareness in your neighbourhood.
